title: Training Module – Western Blotting Data Analysis
summary: The experiment compares the expression and phosphorylation status of wild-type and mutant Protein X in response to ligand treatment using Western blotting.

key_factors.why: To compare the regulation of normal Protein X versus the mutant version.
To observe the impact of the ligand on Protein X activity and phosphorylation.
The total antibody detects overall expression levels, while the phospho-specific antibody isolates the phosphorylation state of Tyr24.
To identify the molecular weight (30 kDa) of Protein X bands.

sequence.step: Treat WT and mutant cell lines with or without ligand
sequence.why: To induce experimental conditions for Protein X regulation.
To extract cellular proteins for analysis.

sequence.step: Load lysates and size marker into gel lanes
sequence.why: To separate proteins by molecular weight.

sequence.step: Perform gel electrophoresis and protein transfer
sequence.why: To move separated proteins from the gel to a membrane for antibody probing.

sequence.step: Probe membrane with anti-Protein X or anti-phospho-Tyr24 antibodies
sequence.why: To specifically detect Protein X or its phosphorylated form.
To analyze Protein X concentration and phosphorylation state.
